action.description: What the character says/does. Visible to the GM and, if performed in front of the other character, to the other player too.

private_reasoning.description: Why the character is doing this, based ONLY on what this character currently knows/believes. Never shown to the other player. Used by God to check for leaked knowledge.

check_requested.description: Section 3 'anything genuinely uncertain' checks only (stealth, forcing a door, reading the unreadable, etc). For combat, use combat_action instead -- don't fill in both for the same action.

combat_action.description: Declare a combat maneuver this turn. The orchestrator resolves it deterministically in dice.py before the GM narrates -- do not use check_requested for anything combat-related.

type.description: attack = a plain swing, always lands (section 3 -- no attack roll either side). parry_attempt = reacting to an enemy's telegraphed heavy this turn (section 9). called_shot = targeting a limb with a stopwatch tax if its multiplier is >1.0 (section 10; only the head qualifies on a standard humanoid). ambush_called_shot = a genuine unaware/stealth opening -- free, no stopwatch, stacks with visceral (section 10).

swing.description: Only meaningful for 'attack' and 'called_shot'. Heavy costs more movement (section 8) and hits harder (section 4 weapon formula).

target.description: Name of the monster or character this action targets.

target_limb.description: Only for 'called_shot'/'ambush_called_shot'. Use the target's own limb naming -- head/torso/arm_left/arm_right/leg_left/leg_right for a player character, or head/torso/arm/leg for a monster (monsters don't track left/right separately in this simulation).
